workflow_rationale: Existing state management is fragmented across multiple files. This refactor consolidates it into a unified StateManager with schema validation and graceful degradation, following the Add New → Migrate → Cleanup pattern to maintain system stability throughout.

phases.name: Add Consolidated StateManager
phases.description: Create a new StateManager class that consolidates all scanner state (active trades, cooldowns, watch state, last scan timestamps) into a unified, schema-validated JSON store. The new system runs alongside the existing fragmented files.

subtasks.description: Create StateManager class with schema-validated JSON storage

verification.command: python3 -c "from src.state_manager import StateManager; sm = StateManager('test'); print('OK' if sm else 'FAIL')"

subtasks.notes: Implement: StateManager class with methods: save_trade_state(), get_trade_state(), save_cooldown(), check_cooldown(), save_watch_state(), get_watch_state(), save_scan_timestamp(), get_scan_timestamp(). Use atomic writes (write to .tmp then rename). Include schema validation with graceful degradation.

subtasks.description: Add consolidated state schema definition and validation

verification.command: python3 -c "from src.state_manager import StateManager; sm = StateManager('test'); sm.validate_schema({'active_trades': {}, 'cooldowns': {}, 'watch_state': {}, 'scan_timestamps': {}}); print('OK')"

subtasks.notes: Schema: {version, active_trades: {symbol: {active, id, side, entry, sl, tp1, tp2, tp1_hit, be_trig, t0, mfe, be_moved}}, cooldowns: {symbol: timestamp}, watch_state: {symbol: {t, price, label}}, scan_timestamps: {symbol: timestamp}}. Validate on load, log warning and use defaults on corruption.

verification.command: python3 -c "from src.state_manager import StateManager; sm = StateManager('test'); sm.set_active_trade('XAUUSD', 'LONG', 2400.0, 2395.0, 2410.0, 2420.0, 12345); t = sm.get_active_trade('XAUUSD'); print('OK' if t and t['side'] == 'LONG' else 'FAIL')"

subtasks.notes: Methods: set_active_trade(symbol, side, entry, sl, tp1, tp2, signal_id, be_trig), get_active_trade(symbol), clear_active_trade(symbol), update_trade_state(symbol, updates), get_all_active_trades()

subtasks.description: Add migration utility to import existing state files

verification.command: echo '{"active": true, "id": 123, "side": "LONG", "entry": 2400}' > /tmp/test_trade.json && python3 -c "from src.state_manager import StateManager; sm = StateManager('test'); sm.import_legacy_state('XAUUSD', '/tmp/test_trade.json', 'trade'); print('OK' if sm.get_active_trade('XAUUSD') else 'FAIL')"

subtasks.notes: Verified import_legacy_state method successfully imports legacy state files for trades, cooldowns, and watch state. Test passed: imported legacy trade file and retrieved active trade data correctly. Migration utility enables seamless transition from fragmented per-file state to consolidated StateManager.

phases.name: Migrate scalp_fast.py to StateManager
phases.description: Update scalp_fast.py to use StateManager for all state operations while keeping old file-based methods as fallback during transition.

subtasks.notes: Completed: Imported StateManager from src/state_manager. Initialized StateManager instance in init_symbol() with per-symbol state file. Replaced set_active_trade() to use state_manager.set_active_trade(SYMBOL, ...). Maintained existing logic for finalizing prior trades using state_manager.get_trade_state().

Completed: Replaced json.load(open(TRADE_STATE)) with state_manager.get_trade_state(SYMBOL). Replaced json.dump with state_manager.save_trade_state(SYMBOL, t). All trade state operations now go through StateManager with proper error handling.

Completed: Removed CD_FILE and WATCH_CD_FILE manual JSON file operations. Replaced cooldown check with state_manager.in_cooldown(SYMBOL) and get_cooldown_remaining(SYMBOL). Replaced watch state management with state_manager.get_watch_state(SYMBOL) and set_watch_state(SYMBOL, data). Replaced cooldown setting with state_manager.set_cooldown(SYMBOL, COOLDOWN_MIN). All cooldown logic now uses StateManager with proper persistence.

subtasks.description: Add scan timestamp tracking to main()

verification.command: python3 scalp_fast.py --symbol XAUUSD && python3 -c "from src.state_manager import StateManager; sm = StateManager('scanner'); ts = sm.get_scan_timestamp('XAUUSD'); print('OK' if ts else 'FAIL')"

subtasks.notes: Completed: Added state_manager.save_scan_timestamp(SYMBOL) before all 14 return points in main() to ensure timestamp is tracked regardless of scan outcome (error, no data, volatility gate, news blackout, cooldown, no setup, DRY mode, REVIEW mode, or successful signal). This ensures scanner health monitoring and orchestrator visibility of last scan times.

phases.name: Migrate scalp_scan.py to StateManager
phases.description: Update scalp_scan.py to use StateManager for signal deduplication state.

subtasks.notes: Completed: Migrated scalp_scan.py to use StateManager. Imported StateManager from src/state_manager. Initialized StateManager instance with 'scalp_scan' namespace. Replaced load_state() to use state_manager.get_watch_state('XAUUSD'). Replaced save_state(k) to use state_manager.save_watch_state('XAUUSD', {'key': k}). Signal deduplication key now persists across scanner restarts. Verified with comprehensive integration tests showing state persistence, duplicate detection, and restart resilience.

verification.command: python3 scalp_scan.py 80 && python3 -c "from src.state_manager import StateManager; sm = StateManager('scanner'); print('OK' if sm.get_scan_timestamp('XAUUSD') else 'FAIL')"

subtasks.notes: Completed: Added StateManager instance with 'scanner' namespace at start of main() function. Added scan_state.save_scan_timestamp('XAUUSD') call at end of main() to record successful scan completion. Timestamp is saved regardless of signal outcome, enabling orchestrator to track scanner health and last scan times.

phases.name: Migrate orchestrate.py to StateManager
phases.description: Update orchestrate.py to use StateManager for active trade detection.

subtasks.notes: Completed: Imported StateManager from src.state_manager. Replaced active_pairs() function to use state_manager.get_trade_state(sym) instead of reading individual JSON files. Function now instantiates StateManager with 'scanner' namespace, iterates through INSTR symbols, and returns list of symbols where trade state exists and active=True. Maintains same functionality with centralized state management.

subtasks.description: Add scan timestamp reporting to orchestrator
subtasks.notes: In main() print loop, add last scan time for each symbol: ts = sm.get_scan_timestamp(sym); if ts: print age in seconds. Helps debug 'why wasn't this symbol scanned?'

phases.name: Integration Testing
phases.description: Test the complete scanner lifecycle with StateManager: fresh start, signal generation, trade tracking, restart, state restoration.

subtasks.description: Test fresh start with no existing state

verification.instructions: 1. Delete ~/.tv_scanner_state.json if exists. 2. Run python3 scalp_fast.py --symbol XAUUSD. 3. Verify clean startup with no errors. 4. Check state file created at ~/.tv_scanner_state.json with valid schema.

subtasks.notes: Verify graceful cold start - scanner should initialize with empty state and create new state file.

verification.instructions: 1. Run scalp_fast.py until a trade signal fires. 2. Verify trade state saved. 3. Kill the scanner (Ctrl+C). 4. Restart scalp_fast.py. 5. Verify active trade is restored and TP/SL management continues. 6. Check no duplicate Telegram alerts sent.

subtasks.notes: This is the PRIMARY use case from the spec - active trade context must survive restart.

subtasks.description: Test corrupted state file graceful degradation

verification.instructions: 1. Manually corrupt ~/.tv_scanner_state.json (invalid JSON or wrong schema). 2. Run scalp_fast.py. 3. Verify warning logged. 4. Verify scanner starts fresh with empty state. 5. Verify no crash.

subtasks.notes: Acceptance criteria: 'Graceful degradation: if state file is corrupted, scanner starts fresh with a warning log'

verification.instructions: 1. Trigger a signal to start cooldown. 2. Immediately kill and restart scanner. 3. Verify cooldown is still active (no duplicate signal within cooldown window). 4. Wait for cooldown to expire. 5. Verify new signal can fire.

subtasks.notes: User story: 'I don't want to receive duplicate Telegram alerts after restarting the scanner'

verification.instructions: 1. Run orchestrate.py to scan all pairs. 2. Verify scan timestamps updated for all instruments. 3. Restart orchestrator. 4. Verify active_pairs() correctly identifies instruments with active trades. 5. Verify last scan times are preserved.

subtasks.notes: Orchestrator must correctly resume multi-instrument state tracking across restarts.

phases.name: Cleanup and Documentation
phases.description: Remove fallback to old fragmented state files, add state schema documentation, verify all old state access patterns are migrated.

verification.command: grep -n 'TRADE_STATE\|CD_FILE\|WATCH_CD_FILE' scalp_fast.py && echo 'FAIL: old patterns found' || echo 'OK'

subtasks.notes: Remove all references to: TRADE_STATE, CD_FILE, WATCH_CD_FILE, TG_STATE direct access. All state must go through StateManager.

verification.instructions: Review STATE_SCHEMA.md for completeness: schema version, all field definitions, example state file, corruption handling, migration notes.

subtasks.notes: Acceptance criteria: 'State file is human-readable JSON with clear schema documentation'. Document: schema structure, field types, default values, TTLs, corruption recovery.

subtasks.description: Add migration script for existing deployments
subtasks.notes: Script to scan for all ~/.tv_fast_* files and import them into consolidated StateManager. Supports --dry-run and --backup flags.

parallel_groups.reason: All three migration phases depend only on phase-1 and modify different files

parallelism.speedup_estimate: Phases 2-4 can run in parallel but are small Python files - sequential is safer

acceptance_criteria: Scanner state is persisted to disk every scan cycle (every 60 seconds)
On restart, scanner loads last state and resumes cooldowns, active signals, and trade management
Duplicate alerts are suppressed for signals that were already sent before the restart
State file is human-readable JSON with clear schema documentation
Graceful degradation: if state file is corrupted, scanner starts fresh with a warning log

verification_steps.name: Manual Restart Test
verification_steps.expected_outcome: Active trade persists across scanner restart

verification_steps.name: Corruption Handling Test
verification_steps.expected_outcome: Scanner starts fresh with warning on corrupted state file

verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk: modifying live trading scanner state. Integration tests + manual validation required to ensure no duplicate alerts or missed TP/SL updates.

steps: Fresh start with no existing state
State persistence across restart with active trade
